technologies.name: Datadog
technologies.evidence: By bringing CDN observability into Datadog, we helped the customer move from fragmented monitoring to a more complete and operationally useful model for digital performance.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions Datadog as the observability platform used to bring CDN observability into a unified model.

technologies.name: Akamai
technologies.evidence: The customer was already using Akamai for content delivery and edge protection.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions Akamai as the CDN provider used by the customer.

technologies.name: Akamai DataStream 2
technologies.evidence: The solution combined several telemetry sources into a single observability framework: Akamai DataStream 2 logs for request, response, cache, geography, and edge delivery visibility.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions Akamai DataStream 2 as a telemetry source used for CDN observability.

technologies.name: Synthetic Monitoring
technologies.evidence: The solution combined several telemetry sources into a single observability framework: Synthetic Monitoring for external validation of availability, DNS, TLS, and critical user journeys.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions Synthetic Monitoring as a telemetry source used for CDN observability.

technologies.name: Real User Monitoring (RUM)
technologies.evidence: The solution combined several telemetry sources into a single observability framework: Real User Monitoring (RUM) to measure actual customer experience across key digital touchpoints.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions RUM as a telemetry source used for CDN observability.
